text: This paper introduces BEAST 2, an open source software framework for Bayesian evolutionary analysis of trees. This application is a highly modular, fully Bayesian, Bayesian MCMC molecular phylogenetics framework, with roots in BEAST 1, but with a new, improved design that addresses the limitations of modularity and maintainability. BEAST 2 has a new, highly modular design with a package manager that allows for easy development and distribution of independent analysis modules. Models can be developed and implemented as plug-in modules that can be easily incorporated into the BEAST 2 framework, without requiring modifications to the core application. New models developed in BEAST 2 are presented, such as advanced birth-death processes, phylodynamic tools and multi-locus species tree inference methods. BEAST 2 forms a highly flexible and robust foundation for the development of many new Bayesian analysis tools for phylogenetics in the years to come.
